  10. Tech ecosystem crystallization is modest (+0.029 modularity), not dramatic. The LLM-vendor ecosystem was already coherent in 2024 pooled. Real novelty: AI dev-tools sub-cluster (Copilot, Cursor, Claude-tool) SPLIT OFF in 2026 — tool use is semantically distinct from systems engineering.

docs.title: Evidence assessment — ranked findings
docs.text: Ordered by (evidence strength) × (novelty) × (narrative value). Each finding carries a strength rating, sample, and V1/V2 verification status.

docs.title: Tier A — Lead-candidate findings (strong, novel, core to the paper)
docs.text:
A1. Cross-occupation employer-vs-worker AI divergence is universal and SWE-specific in magnitude. Evidence: strong (T23 + T32; V2 confirmed DiD robust across 4 alt control definitions). 16/16 subgroups positive gap 2024 AND 2026 under any-AI benchmark. Spearman(exposure, gap) +0.71. SWE DiD +14.02 pp [+13.67, +14.37] vs control +0.17. This is the paper's strongest single quantitative claim. Figure candidate: T32's cross-occupation divergence chart.

A2. Within-firm AI content rewriting is real, same-title, and clean of composition. Evidence: strong (three independent panels converge: T16 +7.7-8.3 pp company-level, T37 +7.91 pp returning-cohort, T31 +10-13.4 pp pair-level). Pair-level EXCEEDS company-level → same-title rewriting beyond title-composition. V1/V2 verified. Exemplar: Microsoft "Software Engineer II" rewrite at n=35 with Copilot/GenAI/AI-Systems citations.

A3. Seniority boundaries sharpened, not blurred. Evidence: strong (T15 TF-IDF 0.950 → 0.871; T20 AUC +0.150 associate↔mid-senior; T12 label-based AND YOE-based relabeling diagnostics agree: 2026 entry ≠ 2024 senior). Falsifies a core RQ1 subclaim. Negative results of this cleanness are publishable.

A4. Scope inflation is universal, senior > junior, within-domain — not junior-specific relabeling. Evidence: strong (T11 S4 breadth +2.61 > J3 +1.58; T28 60-85% within-domain decomposition; T33 rejects hiring-bar-lowering mechanism). V1/V2 verified within-company S4 +1.97 > J3 +1.43.

A5. Two emergent senior archetypes with content-grounded role names. Evidence: strong (T34 cluster 0 Applied-AI/LLM Engineer 15.6× growth, 94% 2026; cluster 1 Tech-orchestration/DevOps 2.6× growth). Title-regex + T09 archetype cross-tab + content exemplars confirm role coherence. V2 confirmed 14/20 cluster 0 title sample explicitly AI/ML.

A6. AI-DiD cross-occupation robustness. Evidence: strong (V2 Phase E). SWE DiD survives dropping data/financial analysts, dropping nurse, restricting to manual-work controls, dropping title_lookup_llm SWE tier. All within 0.5 pp of +13-14 pp. SWE-specificity is robust to classification-tier and control-group choices.

docs.title: Tier B — Strong supporting findings
docs.text:
B1. AI-mention acceleration is 2.80× above within-2024 noise — the cleanest rate signal. Evidence: strong (T19 + calibration_table.csv). Only AI (ratio 2.80) and scope (1.54) clear the > 1 threshold. Seniority annualized rates are dominated by within-2024 noise; report raw 2024→2026 pp deltas (T30 panel), not annualized seniority rates.

B2. Management language is flat, not falling — a measurement correction. Evidence: strong (V1 exposed 0.28 precision on mgmt_broad; T21 applied V1-rebuilt pattern and found senior mgmt density FLAT; V2 verified). Corrects T11 claim. Publishable as a methods-caveat on longitudinal posting-content studies.

B3. Hiring-bar-lowering hypothesis REJECTED. Evidence: strong (T33 classifier-sensitive magnitude, but hiring-bar-proxy correlations uniformly |ρ| ≤ 0.28, within-company cross-metric ≈ 0, 0/50 narrative-content samples contain explicit requirement-loosening language).

B4. Sampling-frame-mediated composition NOT dominant — 13/15 headlines robust on returning cohort. Evidence: strong (T37). Paper can proceed without a sampling-frame caveat as first-order qualification. J3 and S4 directions INTENSIFY on returning-only.

B5. Hiring-selectivity hypothesis REJECTED. Evidence: strong (T38). Content shifts NOT concentrated in volume-contracting firms; volume-UP firms write longer JDs. JOLTS trough is real but NOT the mechanism.

B6. LLM-authorship NOT dominant mediator. Evidence: strong (T29). Content effects persist at 80-130% on low-authorship-score subset. Length growth ~half LLM-mediated (boilerplate); AI-mention, credential stacking, scope inflation, CI/CD rise are real.

B7. AI dev-tools sub-cluster SPLIT OFF in 2026 as semantically distinct from LLM engineering. Evidence: moderate-strong (T35 Louvain stability confirmed). Copilot, Cursor, ChatGPT-tool, Claude-tool, Codex with ≥⅘ having no 2024 above-threshold edges. Tool use vs systems engineering are separate dimensions in the 2026 tech taxonomy.

B8. CI/CD is the single largest S4 riser (+20.6 pp), bigger than any AI term. Evidence: moderate (T14 + T15). Orchestration is the content backbone of senior-tier shift.

docs.title: Tier C — Supporting / methodological contributions
docs.text:
C1. Technology ecosystem modularity rose modestly (+0.029). T35. Real but not dramatic. LLM-vendor ecosystem was already coherent in 2024 pooled; 2026 added members. The TRUE novelty is AI dev-tools sub-cluster (B7).

C2. Geographic AI rise is UNIFORM; ML/LLM archetype is DIFFUSING to non-hubs. T17. AI-rise leaders are Atlanta/Tampa/Miami/SLC, not SF/Seattle. Metro-level J3 and AI changes are decoupled (r = −0.22).

C3. Aggregators post CLEANER JDs than direct employers. T22. Counter-prior; useful methodological finding for posting-study scholarship.

C4. Copilot at 0.10% of postings. T23. Employers do NOT formalize even the most-adopted AI tool. Explains part of the employer-worker divergence: under-specification in JDs.

C5. Legacy substitution is LEGACY → MODERN STACK, not LEGACY → AI. T36 rejects the legacy-to-AI-ification framing. Stack modernization dominates.

C6. The T30 multi-operationalization seniority panel framework. Methods contribution: 12 of 13 definitions direction-consistent across the exploration. Junior-side UP under all 7 junior definitions; senior-side DOWN under 5 of 6 senior definitions (S2 director-only flat). Paper should publish the panel as a reusable tool.

C7. Within-2024 calibration SNR framework. Methods contribution: per-metric SNR (cross-period effect / within-2024 effect). AI-strict SNR 32.9; scope 42.8; seniority shares near/below noise for annualized rates (use raw pp deltas instead).

C8. V1 pattern-validation methodology exposed 0.28/0.55 precision on widely-used management patterns. Methods contribution. Longitudinal posting studies need semantic (not tautological) precision validation.

docs.title: Tier D — Flagged / demoted findings
docs.text:
D1. "Junior requirements section chars shrank" (T13). Classifier-sensitive (direction flips under simple-regex), authorship-sensitive (near-disappears on low-LLM subset). Demoted to qualified claim: "junior descriptions densified on AI/tech tokens; net requirements-section direction is classifier-uncertain."

D2. "Aggregate credential stack depth rose." Below within-2024 noise (SNR 0.59). Cite only per-seniority claims (J3 +16.9 pp, S4 +13.3 pp) — do NOT cite aggregate.

D3. MCP in ai_broad pattern for 2024 baselines. "Microsoft Certified Professional" contamination. Use ai_strict for MCP-specific growth citations.

D4. T16/T23 pattern-provenance mismatch. V2 flagged that T16 and T23 numbers match top-level ai_strict (0.86 precision) rather than V1-rebuilt ai_strict_v1_rebuilt (0.96 precision). Under V1-rebuilt, magnitudes drop ~10-15% but direction unchanged. Gate 3 note for SYNTHESIS.md: cite ai_strict with its 0.86 precision or re-derive under V1-rebuilt for cleaner numbers. Since direction holds, the paper's qualitative story is unaffected.

D5. T31 pair-count panel-dependent. V2 replicates +10-13 pp on different pair constructions (n=12 strict arshkon-only, n=23 primary, n=37 relaxed). Direction (pair > company) robust; exact magnitude range-report.

docs.title: Seniority panel — final verdict
docs.text:
T30's 12-of-13 direction-consistency held through Wave 3.5. Primary remains J3 / S4 with pooled-2024 baseline; arshkon-only co-primary for senior claims. The asaniczka senior-side asymmetry (+7.1 pp within-2024 on S4) is real and persistent; senior claims MUST cite both pooled (−7.6 pp) and arshkon-only (−1.8 pp) magnitudes. S2 director-only is flat (noise) and should NOT appear as a lead claim.

T20's supervised AUC corroborates T15's unsupervised TF-IDF sharpening: entry↔associate AUC +0.093, associate↔mid-senior AUC +0.150, mid-senior↔director softened −0.022 (driven by director share moving toward the AI-oriented senior cluster T34 identified).

Panel-consistent SYNTHESIS.md claims: - J3 (YOE ≤ 2) entry-share +5.0 pp pooled (+1.2 pp arshkon-only) — direction UP robust across J1/J2/J3/J4. - S4 (YOE ≥ 5) senior-share −7.6 pp pooled (−1.8 pp arshkon-only) — direction DOWN robust across S1/S3/S4/S5; S2 flat. - J3 scope metrics (breadth_resid, stack depth, tech count) rose significantly — direction robust. - S4 scope metrics rose MORE than J3 — direction robust, magnitude robust.

docs.title: Narrative evaluation — RQ1-RQ4 status
docs.text:
RQ1 — Employer-side restructuring (junior rung narrowing, scope inflation, senior redefinition). - "Junior share reduces" — contradicted (J3 rose +5 pp). - "Junior scope inflates" — partially supported but not junior-specific (T11/T28: universal, senior > junior). - "Junior roles relabeled as senior" — contradicted (T15 + T20 + T12 YOE-based relabeling diagnostic). - "Senior redefinition toward AI orchestration" — supported, with emergent-role specificity (T34 cluster 0 Applied-AI, cluster 1 Data-Platform/DevOps). - "Senior shift AWAY from management" — contradicted under V1-validated pattern (T21 flat); corrected narrative is "toward orchestration, not away from management."

Revised RQ1 claim (for paper): "SWE postings restructured around technology domain and AI-enabled orchestration content. Seniority boundaries sharpened; scope broadened universally with senior-tier larger shifts; an emergent Applied-AI/LLM Engineer archetype grew 15.6×."

RQ2 — Task and requirement migration. - "Requirements migrate downward across seniority" — contradicted in part. T15 junior-senior divergence + T20 sharpening + Gate 2 copilot rate (J3 4.6% ≈ S4 4.1%) show AI is at both levels rather than migrating. The correct framing is "AI requirements rose universally across seniority; orchestration content rose more at senior." - "Migration into YOE ≤ 2 postings" — supported with qualification (T28 within-domain J3 scope rose; but so did S4 scope, by more).

Revised RQ2 claim: "AI tools appear at junior and senior postings at comparable rates; orchestration and platform-infrastructure content concentrated at senior postings. There is no downward migration; there is universal scope broadening with senior-tier larger magnitudes."

RQ3 — Employer-requirement / worker-usage divergence. - STRONGLY SUPPORTED. T23 + T32: 16/16 subgroups positive gap in 2024 AND 2026, Spearman(exposure, gap) +0.71, SWE DiD +14 pp vs control +0.17 pp. This is the paper's potential lead claim.

RQ3 survives intact and strengthens to "universal across AI-exposed occupations."

RQ4 — Mechanisms (interviews). Out of scope for computational exploration. T29 + T33 + T38 pre-cleared alternative explanations (LLM-authorship mediation, hiring-bar-lowering, hiring-selectivity) by REJECTING them at the quantitative level. RQ4 interviews can focus on: how senior engineers experience the orchestration shift; how hiring-side actors explain the SWE-specific AI concentration; why employers under-specify tool asks (Copilot 0.10%); whether "Applied-AI/LLM Engineer" is experienced as a new role or a rebranding.

docs.title: Emerging narrative (final pre-synthesis form)
docs.text:
Proposed paper abstract (2-3 sentences):

Between 2024 and 2026, software-engineering job postings underwent a real, same-firm, same-title content rewriting concentrated on AI tooling and platform-infrastructure orchestration. The rewriting is SWE-specific (DiD +14 pp against control occupations; universal across 16/16 tested subgroups), within-firm (same-company × same-title pair-level AI drift +10-13 pp exceeds company-level +8 pp), and independent of recruiter-LLM authorship style, sampling-frame shifts, and hiring-market selectivity. Seniority boundaries sharpened rather than blurred, scope broadened universally with senior-tier shifts larger than junior, and a new Applied-AI/LLM Engineer archetype grew 15.6× — a structural feature of the 2026 SWE hiring market rather than a junior-level relabeling or composition effect.

Alternative framings still worth weighing at SYNTHESIS: 1. Dataset/methods lead: the T30 panel, the SNR calibration framework, the pattern-validation methodology (V1's 0.28 precision exposure), and the composition-decomposition framework are independently publishable as a methods paper. 2. Cross-occupation employer-worker divergence lead: if the paper emphasizes the universality (16/16 subgroups) rather than the within-firm SWE-specific story, it becomes a general labor-market paper on anticipatory AI-hiring signaling. 3. SWE-restructuring empirical lead (preferred): the above abstract.

The orchestrator recommends positioning #3 with #1 as methods contribution in the middle third of the paper.

docs.title: Research question evolution (final)
docs.text:
RQ1 → revised: How did SWE posting content restructure 2024→2026 across seniority, technology domain, and within-vs-between-firm dimensions? What is the content signature of within-firm rewriting?

RQ1a (new): Do seniority boundaries blur, sharpen, or stay stable? Answer: sharpened (T15 + T20).

RQ1b (new): Is scope change within-seniority, cross-seniority flow, or universal? Answer: universal, senior > junior (T11 + T28).

RQ1c (new): Did new senior archetypes emerge? Answer: yes, two — Applied-AI/LLM Engineer + Data-Platform/DevOps Engineer (T34).

RQ2 → revised: Which requirement categories moved between seniority levels, and which moved universally? Answer: AI tools universal, not downward; orchestration concentrated at senior; no management exodus (management flat under valid patterns).

RQ3 → affirmed and extended: Does employer AI-requirement rate diverge from worker AI-usage rate? Is this pattern SWE-specific or universal across AI-exposed occupations? Answer: divergence universal across 16/16 subgroups; SWE DiD +14 pp vs control +0.17 pp.

RQ4: unchanged (interview-based).

New post-3.5 RQ candidate:

RQ5 (induced): Does within-firm same-title rewriting explain the bulk of 2024→2026 SWE content shifts, after controlling for composition, sampling-frame, authorship, and hiring selectivity? Answer: yes (T16 + T31 + T37 + T38 + T29).

docs.title: Gaps and weaknesses
docs.text:
1. Pattern-provenance mismatch in T16/T23 (V2 D4). Numbers cite top-level ai_strict (0.86 precision) not V1-rebuilt (0.96). Direction holds; magnitude drops ~10-15% under rebuilt. Mitigation in SYNTHESIS.md: footnote the pattern-precision trade-off; cite the direction as primary claim.

  2. T31 pair-count range (V2 D5). +10-13 pp across constructions. Mitigation: range-report in SYNTHESIS.md.

  3. Requirements-section Δ classifier-sensitive (T13 + T33 + V2 confirmed). Direction flips under alternative classifier. Mitigation: cite as a flagged finding; emphasize that length growth is boilerplate-led is direction-robust while requirements-section change is classifier-dependent.

  4. T36 thin disappearing-titles list (n=2-11). Legacy-substitution story is small-sample. Mitigation: cite as a negative / qualified result — "we find no evidence that legacy-role substitution carries AI-ification."

  5. Control-occupation definition sensitivity. V2 tested 4 alt definitions, all held. Analysis-phase can pre-register a narrower / wider definition.

  6. Asaniczka-only 2024 senior-baseline is +7.1 pp higher on S4 than arshkon-only. Persistent across LLM-YOE. Mitigation: always cite both pooled and arshkon-only magnitudes for senior claims.

  7. Industry taxonomy drift (LinkedIn 2024→2026). No cross-period industry-stratified claim is valid at raw-label level. Analysis-phase needs a cross-taxonomy mapping.

  8. posting_age_days coverage is 0.9%. Posting-lifecycle analysis is not feasible with current data. Analysis-phase either drops this or requires a different data source.

  9. Benchmark sensitivity for T32/T23. Worker-AI-usage benchmarks are heterogeneous (survey → "tried ever"; DORA → "daily"; Anthropic → task coverage). T32 reported under 4 bands; direction robust across all. But magnitudes vary substantially. Mitigation: paper should present the RANGE, not a point estimate.

  10. ML Engineer cross-source classification gap (T04: arshkon 78% SWE vs asaniczka 59%). Mitigation is source-stratification which Wave 3+ applied. Persists as an analysis-phase caveat.

docs.title: Direction for Wave 4 — Agent N's SYNTHESIS.md production
docs.text: Agent N is about to execute T24 (hypothesis consolidation) + T25 (interview artifacts) + T26 (SYNTHESIS.md). This memo is the primary input. Specific guidance:

docs.title: T24 — Hypothesis consolidation
docs.text:
Directly tested in Wave 3.5: - H_A (cross-occupation divergence): STRONGLY SUPPORTED. T32 universal across 16/16 subgroups. - H_B (hidden hiring-bar): REJECTED. T33 classifier-sensitive direction, no correlation with YOE/stack/tech/education. - H_C (emergent senior archetype): STRONGLY SUPPORTED. T34 two clusters with content-grounded names. - H_H (sampling-frame artifact): REJECTED. T37 13/15 headlines robust. - H_K (ecosystem crystallization): PARTIALLY SUPPORTED. T35 modularity +0.029 modest; AI dev-tools sub-cluster split off is the clearer novelty. - H_L (legacy → AI substitution): REJECTED. T36 legacy → modern stack, not AI. - H_M (same-co same-title drift): SUPPORTED. T31 pair-level > company-level (direction robust). - H_N (hiring-selectivity × scope): REJECTED. T38 no correlation; positive if any.

Deferred to analysis phase (from T24 planned list): - H_D (senior IC-as-team-multiplier) — analysis-phase, requires within-firm panel regression. - H_E (same-co J1 drop + J3 rise regime shift) — analysis-phase, requires formal break analysis. - H_F (Sunbelt AI surge catchup) — T17 found geographic uniformity; H_F is partially absorbed but formal catchup-timing needs event-study. - H_G (staff-title redistribution) — T10 found "staff" doubled, "senior" dropped 12 pp; analysis-phase can formalize. - H_I (AI as coordination signal) — needs mechanism-test design. - H_J (recruiter-LLM senior bias) — T29 rejected the general-mediation hypothesis; H_J specifically targets senior postings.

New post-3.5 hypothesis candidates: - H_O: "Within-firm AI rewriting intensity correlates with firm's 2024 digital-transformation maturity" — requires external firm-maturity data. - H_P: "Applied-AI Engineer archetype concentrates in financial services due to regulatory-compliance AI adoption" — T34 found 17% financial services over-representation.

docs.title: T25 — Interview artifacts
docs.text: Strongest exemplars (drawn from existing reports): - Microsoft "Software Engineer II" pair-level rewrite with Copilot/GenAI/AI-Systems citations (T31). - Wells Fargo, Capital One top-20 AI-drift exemplars (T31). - 3-5 entry-level ghost postings with ≥3 senior scope terms (T22). - Applied-AI/LLM Engineer cluster 0 exemplars (T34; 20 samples). These 20 are the single most important interview prompt — show real posting text to senior engineers, ask "is this a real job?" - T23 cross-occupation divergence chart. - T32 cross-occupation divergence figure (paper-lead candidate). - T15 junior-senior centroid movement figure (falsifies relabeling hypothesis).

docs.title: T26 — SYNTHESIS.md
docs.text:
Build on this Gate 3 memo. Ranked findings above = Tier A core + Tier B supporting + Tier C methods + Tier D flagged. Robustness appendix = T37 retention-ratio table + T38 selectivity null + T29 low-LLM-subset re-test + V1/V2 magnitude corrections + T30 panel direction-consistency. Method recommendations for analysis phase = pattern-validation protocol, T30 panel, SNR calibration, within-vs-between decomposition, pair-level drift construction.

Paper positioning recommendation (for Agent N): hybrid empirical/methods paper with the following chapter structure: 1. Introduction framed around the employer-worker AI-requirement divergence (T23 + T32). 2. Dataset + methods (T30 panel, SNR, pattern validation, within-vs-between). 3. Empirical section 1: Technology-domain restructuring and Applied-AI Engineer emergence. 4. Empirical section 2: Universal scope broadening with sharpened seniority boundaries. 5. Empirical section 3: Within-firm AI rewriting at same-title level (pair-level exemplars). 6. Robustness: sampling-frame, authorship, selectivity, classifier, panel variants. 7. Alternative explanations adjudicated (hiring-bar-lowering REJECTED, legacy-to-AI REJECTED, hiring-selectivity REJECTED). 8. Discussion: what this implies for "AI restructures the SWE labor market" claims; what interviews should investigate next (RQ4).

docs.title: Current paper positioning — final Gate 3 recommendation
docs.text:
Strongest paper: a hybrid empirical + methods paper with the cross-occupation AI-divergence finding (A1) and the within-firm same-title rewriting (A2) as co-lead claims, supported by the sharpened seniority boundaries (A3) and the Applied-AI Engineer archetype emergence (A5). The methods contributions (T30 panel, SNR calibration, pattern validation, within-vs-between) are a clean 25-30% of the paper's contribution and independently publishable as methods-venue content.

Competing positioning #2: substantive labor-market paper focused narrowly on cross-occupation divergence (A1 + A6). Simpler narrative; would lose the within-firm and archetype findings to appendix. Not recommended.

Competing positioning #3: technology-ecosystem restructuring paper (T09 + T14 + T34 + T35). Technology-domain axis as primary. Methodologically sound but less novelty vs prior labor papers. Not recommended as lead, but absorbed into empirical section 1.

The negative / null findings (H_B rejected, H_L rejected, H_M/H_N rejected, H_H rejected, T29 rejected, management flat) are a major strength of the paper — they rule out the leading alternative explanations for the AI rise. SYNTHESIS.md must foreground these as "we tested and rejected X, Y, Z" rather than hiding them.

Orchestrator note: Agent N's SYNTHESIS.md is the paper's analytical backbone; this memo is its primary input. Gate 3 is done.

docs.title: Exploration Task Index
docs.text: Run start: 2026-04-20 Pipeline: Wave 1 → Wave 1.5 → Wave 2 → V1 → Wave 3 → Wave 3.5 → V2 → Wave 4 → Wave 5

docs.title: Status legend
docs.text:
• ⏳ pending
  • 🏃 in progress
  • ✅ completed
  • ⚠️ flagged (see notes)
  • ❌ failed

docs.title: Wave 1 — Data Foundation
docs.text: Task Owner Status Report Key finding / note T01. Data profile & column coverage Agent A ✅ T01.md 68,137 SWE LinkedIn rows; seniority_final='entry' on arshkon SWE is 3.8% vs seniority_native='entry' 15.7% — T30 panel is critical; company_industry/company_size/skills_raw have asaniczka holes T02. Asaniczka associate as junior proxy Agent A ✅ T02.md Not usable as pooled junior proxy: matches arshkon entry on only 2 of 4 signals (Jaccard 0.43 + YOE yes; title cues + LLM re-read no — 35% mid-senior under LLM). Downstream must use T30 J2 or YOE-based J3/J4, not an asaniczka-associate→entry substitution. T03. Seniority label audit (seniority_final) Agent B ✅ T03.md seniority_final defensible; LLM abstains on 34-53% of SWE rows; director cell accuracy 22-27%; prefer YOE primaries (J3/S4) downstream. T30. Seniority definition ablation panel Agent B ✅ T30.md 12/13 definitions direction-consistent (7/7 junior UP, ⅚ senior DOWN; S2 flat). J3/S4 confirmed primaries. Senior-side: asaniczka asymmetry persists under S4 (7.1pp within-2024 vs 7.6pp cross-period) — arshkon-only-vs-scraped drops to −1.8pp, report both magnitudes. T04. SWE classification audit Agent B ✅ T04.md is_swe defensible (zero dual-flag violations; 80.6% rule-LLM agreement). Hard FP ~10.9% concentrated in genuinely ambiguous systems/test/reliability engineer titles. ML Engineer has 19.6pp within-2024 gap (arshkon 78% vs asaniczka 59% SWE) — stratify by source for ML claims. Use within-SWE shares; do NOT compare raw SWE share 2024→2026 (scraped is query-stratified). T05. Cross-dataset comparability Agent C ✅ T05.md Aggregate seniority_final near-identical arshkon↔scraped (V=0.022); seniority_native mid-senior share drifts +15–40pp on same titles 2024→2026 with YOE flat — platform relabeling artifact. Description length +1750 chars 2024→2026 (boilerplate + content). Geographic shift (CA +8pp) mostly scraper design. Use J3 (YOE≤2) as primary entry measure; avoid seniority_native-only trends and raw state shares. T06. Company concentration deep investigation Agent C ✅ T06.md Entry posting is minority activity: 58–63% of ≥5-post companies have ZERO J3 entry rows. Overlap panel (n=125): J3 entry rise +5pp 2024→2026 is entirely BETWEEN-company; within-company ≈ 0. AI mention rise +39pp is mostly within-company (+31pp). Duplicate-template audit clean post-Stage-4. Artifacts produced: entry_specialist_employers.csv (206 rows) and returning_companies_cohort.csv (2,109 rows, 55% of 2026 postings). T07. External benchmarks & power analysis Agent D ✅ T07.md Wave 2 primary = J3 (YOE≤2) + S4 (YOE≥5) with pooled 2024 baseline; J1 and S2 underpowered; BLS OES state r=0.94 log-log (pooled); 2026 Info openings at 0.71× 2023 avg — frame everything as shares.

docs.title: Wave 1.5 — Shared Preprocessing
docs.text: Task Owner Status Report Key finding / note Shared preprocessing artifacts Agent Prep ✅ artifacts/shared/README.md 10 artifacts built. Cleaned text 68,137 rows (70.8% LLM text; scraped 56% LLM / 44% raw). Embeddings 48,223 × 384 (MiniLM). Tech matrix 68,137 × 135 (escape-fix verified for C++/C#/.NET). Calibration: AI-strict SNR=32.9, scope-terms SNR=42.8 (clean cross-period); seniority shares near/below noise (Gate 1 asaniczka asymmetry). 23 of 135 techs flagged — all interpretable as real adoption (LLM-era growth / new-in-2026 / legacy decline), no tokenization bugs.

docs.title: Wave 2 — Open Structural Discovery
docs.text: Task Owner Status Report Key finding / note T08. Distribution profiling & anomaly detection Agent E ✅ ⚠️ T08.md Gate 1 "J3 within-co ≈ 0" does NOT replicate outside arshkon-only n=125 panel — pooled / asaniczka / min-3 / min-10 panels show +3.5 to +6.4 pp within-co J3 rise. Senior-SWE-titled J3 DROPS (−1.8 pp) while generic SWE J3 RISES (+7.3 pp). Cap-20 cuts aggregate J3 Δ in half (5.0 → 2.4 pp). Arshkon native='entry' is NOT clean (median YOE=4, 45% ≥5 YOE) — retire from sanity duty. AI-strict +13.3 pp (SNR 32.9, only 7.7% length-driven, equal across labeled/not_selected). 16 anomalies flagged. V1 ⚠️: confirms both arshkon-only ~0 and pooled +3.95 pp within-co; defensible primary is "report BOTH panels", not a single collapsed number. T09. Posting archetype discovery (methods lab) Agent F ✅ T09.md DOMINANT-AXIS: title_archetype / tech_domain (NOT seniority, NOT period). NMI(bert_label, title_arch)=0.216 ≫ NMI(bert_label, seniority_3level)=0.025 (8.8×). ML/LLM archetype is biggest gainer (+4.8pp, cluster 1.65%→6.47%); AI-agent-orchestration a new 2026 archetype (2→45 rows, 10× growth); DoD clearance biggest shrinker (−2.1pp). NMF k=12 deterministic (ARI=0.998); BERTopic seed-unstable (mean ARI=0.24, 46.8% noise). Artifact: swe_archetype_labels.parquet (8,000 rows). Paper narrative pivots to technology-ecosystem restructuring. T10. Title taxonomy evolution Agent G ✅ T10.md Title space NOT fragmenting (unique-per-1K flat ~470-490); but head rotated: AI-token title share 9.6% → 23.5% (+13.9 pp), ai engineer n=2 → n=240 (120×); new 2026-only patterns (founding/forward-deployed); "senior" title-marker share DROPPED 12 pp (39% → 27%) while "staff" doubled (2.7% → 5.5%) and "none" rose 12 pp; TF-IDF cosine for shared titles (0.75-0.86) is only slightly below within-2024 floor (0.77-0.89) — aggregate same-title content is stable. Disappearing-titles list (Wave 3.5 T36 input): n=2 strict (arshkon ≥10 / scraped <3: legacy .NET/Java architect); n=11 wider arshkon≥5. Category shifts: ai_engineering +4.1 pp, data_engineering +2.3 pp, frontend_fullstack +2.7 pp; qa_testing −1.6 pp and embedded_firmware −0.8 pp are the clearest declines. T11. Requirements complexity & credential stacking Agent G ✅ ⚠️ T11.md Length-residualized requirement_breadth rose +1.22 aggregate / +1.58 for J3 entry / +2.61 for S4 senior (residualized). Credential_stack_depth share ≥ 5 categories: J3 +16.9 pp (43% → 60%), S4 +13.3 pp (52% → 66%) — stacking question YES for all seniority cuts. Tech_count (r=0.12 with length) rose +2.06 techs aggregate / +1.64 for J3 (SNR 6.4, strong). AI-strict binary: +13.1 pp aggregate, SNR 28.8. Management density FELL (mgmt_strong_density -0.02, mgmt_broad_density -0.04) — leadership language is not length-normalized-denser in 2026. Precision validation: performance_review, eng_manager (pooled-2024), and all 4 broad-tier terms fail 0.80 — recommend using mgmt_strong minus those. Entry-specialist exclusion + company-cap {20,50} sensitivities move J3 breadth by ≤0.1 items. Outlier top-1% AI-binary went 2.9% → 57.4% (financial-services dominated 2026 outliers: JPM/Citi/GEICO/Visa/Wells/Solera). Shared artifact T11_posting_features.parquet (68,137 × 20) written. V1 ⚠️: confirmed breadth, residualization, phi & tech_count; aggregate credential_stack_depth (+0.20, SNR 0.59) is below noise — strip from aggregate narrative. mgmt_strict 0.55 precision (hire sub-pat 0.07); mgmt_broad 0.28 — T11's mgmt findings are diagnostic-only; rebuilt pattern in validated_mgmt_patterns.json. T12. Open-ended text evolution Agent H ✅ ⚠️ T12.md Period-dominant, not relabeling. 2026 top distinguishing terms: workflows (+28.3z), environments (+26.5), pipelines (+24.3), llm (+14.8), agent (+14.5), observability (+18.1). Accelerating 3×+: rag 75×, multimodal 31×, mcp 29×, multi-agent 24×, agent 17×, genai 15×, ai-powered 16×, embeddings 20×. Section-filtered (reqresp only) top terms nearly identical → AI-shift is in requirements/responsibilities, not boilerplate. Label-based and YOE-based relabeling diagnostics AGREE: 2026 entry ≠ 2024 senior (period-shift, not relabeling). Legacy decline (scala/cobol/puppet) is real but much smaller magnitude than AI growth. Within-2024 calibration: AI terms clean, generic infra (backend/pipelines/cd) noisy. C++ is tokenization artifact in cleaned text. V1 ⚠️: accel ratios exactly verified (RAG 75.3×, multimodal 31.1×, MCP 28.8×, multi-agent 23.6×). But MCP in ai_broad 2024 is Microsoft Certified Professional cert — contamination for any MCP-growth claim citing ai_broad. Use ai_strict only for MCP. T12 did not include entry-specialist exclusion sensitivity per Gate 1 pre-commit 5. T13. Linguistic & structural evolution Agent H ✅ ⚠️ T13.md BOILERPLATE-LED, not requirements. Desc length +1,130 chars (+30%) 2024→2026. Requirements share DROPS −3.5pp (SNR 0.45, below noise); responsibilities +49% absolute (+2.8pp share); benefits section nearly doubled (+89%, +3.9pp share); legal/EEO +80%. J3 junior requirements shrank −5% in absolute chars. Imperative density −19%, inclusive +14%. Gate-1 scope-inflation narrative does not survive: length growth is boilerplate + responsibilities-detail, not requirements-stacking. V1 ⚠️: J3 numbers exactly verified using T13 classifier (reqs 1057→1000, -5.4%). BUT alt-classifier (V1 simpler regex) gave +88% reqs delta on J3 — H3 is classifier-sensitive. Gate 2 must cite "under T13 classifier definition". All-SWE requirements delta -3.5 pp has SNR 0.45 (below noise) — cannot be cited as a point estimate; only directional. T14. Technology ecosystem mapping Agent I ✅ T14.md AI-family crystallizing (pinecone×weaviate phi 0→0.71; rag×llm 0.20→0.51). 60 rising vs 12 declining techs under within-2024 calibration. AI-strict 2026 postings have 48 % higher tech density after length-normalization (additive not substitutive). Stack diversity rising across J3/S4 (median 4→6 J3, 5→7 S4). SQL, C#/.NET, agile declining SNR≥2. Structured-vs-extracted Spearman r=0.967. T15. Semantic similarity landscape Agent I ✅ T15.md Junior-senior DIVERGING, not converging (TF-IDF cosine 0.946→0.863 2024→2026). Period dominates over seniority; within-2024 arshkon-vs-asaniczka SNR ≈1.7-1.8. 2026-junior postings retrieve 2024-junior neighbors 2.6-2.8× over base rate; senior neighbors at base rate — falsifies "junior rewritten as senior" framing. Groups are MORE tightly clustered in 2026 (stylistic homogenization). Robust across embedding + TF-IDF.

docs.title: Gate 2 Verification
docs.text: Task Owner Status Report Key finding / note V1. Gate 2 adversarial verification Agent V1 ✅ V1_verification.md All 6 headlines (H1-H6) verified within 5% including T14 phi crystallization. Main flags: (1) mgmt_broad fails semantic precision at 0.28 (ALL 4 broad tokens below 0.80); mgmt_strict 0.55 (hire=0.07, performance_review=0.25 are worst). (2) ai_broad MCP in 2024 is Microsoft Certified Professional cert — contamination for any MCP-growth claim using ai_broad. (3) Tension A resolved: arshkon-only n=125 within-J3=~0 AND pooled_min5 within-J3=+3.95 pp are BOTH correct; describe different subsamples. (4) H3 J3 requirements -5% is classifier-dependent — alt-classifier gave +88%. Validated patterns artifact written.

docs.title: Wave 3 — Market Dynamics & Cross-cutting Patterns
docs.text: Task Owner Status Report Key finding / note T16. Company hiring strategy typology Agent J ✅ ⚠️ T16.md Panels 243/125/356 replicate V1. J3 within-co panel-sensitive (−0.12 pp arshkon-min5 vs +4.47 pp pooled-min5). AI within-co +7.7–8.3 pp dominates aggregate rise; between-co NEGATIVE. Senior scope > junior on same-co (breadth_resid S4 +2.32 vs J3 +1.43, scope_terms S4 +0.49 vs J3 +0.34 on pooled). New entrants are LESS junior-heavy than returning (12.8% vs 15.3%) — between-co J3 rise is EXIT-driven, not entry-driven. 5 strategy clusters; ai_forward_scope_inflator 38% aggregators. Aggregator-exclusion moves numbers <20%. Gate 1's "+31 pp within-firm AI" replaced by V1-validated +7.7–8.3 pp. V2 ⚠️: pattern-label mismatch — report claims ai_strict_v1_rebuilt (0.96 precision) but code uses top-level ai_strict (0.86); numbers match top-level pattern exactly. Under V1-rebuilt, within-co drops to +7.5-7.8 pp. T17. Geographic market structure Agent J ✅ T17.md 26 metros ≥50/era. AI rise geographically UNIFORM (median SNR 10, all metros positive +4.7–14.5 pp); J3 rise HETEROGENEOUS (−5.1 pp Detroit to +14.9 pp San Diego) with SNR<2 on 13 of 18 calibrated metros. AI-rise leaders are Atlanta/Tampa/Miami/SLC — NOT SF/Seattle. Metro-level AI Δ × J3 Δ r=−0.22 — two phenomena geographically decoupled. Tech-hub vs non-hub premium small (<2 pp). Remote is 19.9% of 2026 scraped (0% 2024 artifact); remote AI 11.1% barely above metro 10.5%. ML/LLM archetype diffusing into non-hubs (Minneapolis +13 pp > Seattle +8.8 pp). Archetype HHI rose in every metro — geographic complement to T09 domain-dominance. Aggregator-exclusion and cap-20 move metro deltas <1 pp. T18. Cross-occupation boundary analysis Agent K ⏳ — — T19. Temporal patterns & rate-of-change Agent K ⏳ — — T20. Seniority boundary clarity Agent L ✅ T20.md Boundaries sharpened on supervised features, corroborating T15 unsupervised result. Entry↔associate AUC +0.093, associate↔mid-senior +0.150; mid-senior↔director softened −0.022 (directors look more like mid-seniors). YOE×period interaction significant (p<1e-44) on requirement_breadth (+0.27 items/YOE-year) and tech_count (+0.18 items/YOE-year); null on ai_binary (rose uniformly across YOE). Δgap J3/S4 primary: attribution_senior > 0.5 for scope_density, ai_binary, description_length, requirement_breadth — "senior > junior scope inflation" confirmed. Missing-middle: associate became MORE distinct from both neighbors (+0.11 entry, +0.23 mid-senior) — carving own identity, not disappearing. Sharpening survives YOE-drop sensitivity. Centroid cosine on z-features: entry↔mid-senior flipped +0.43 → −0.18; mid-senior↔director converged +0.50 → +0.91. T21. Senior role evolution deep dive Agent L ✅ T21.md V1 mgmt_strict_v1_rebuilt validated at 1.00 precision on fresh 50-row sample (target 0.85, PASS). Under rebuilt pattern, senior mgmt_density is FLAT 0.039→0.038 — T11 "management fell" does NOT reproduce. orch validated 0.70 (borderline), strat validated 0.32 (stakeholder=collaboration word, T22 must rebuild). 5-cluster k-means on senior cohort: Low-profile-generic −23.6 pp (62%→38%); Tech-orch-non-AI +11.4 pp; AI-oriented +10.2 pp (15.6× growth, new-at-scale archetype); People-management +1.9 pp; Strategic-language flat. AI-mentioning senior postings are LESS management-heavy, MORE orch-heavy. Director orch +110%, AI-strict 1.1%→14.8%. Cross-seniority mgmt: flat at all tiers under rebuilt pattern — management migration NOT supported. Senior evolution is orchestration-driven, not management-driven. cluster_assignments.csv (33,693 rows) saved for Wave 3.5 T34. T22. Ghost & aspirational requirements Agent M ✅ T22.md Kitchen-sink score rose 2.6× (6.7 → 17.4; non-length artifact). J3 yoe-scope mismatch 2.9% → 7.5% (2.6×). AI tokens sit near hedging 50% of the time vs 28% far-from-AI → "AI asks as wish-list". Aggregators are CLEANER than direct (ghost-likeness concentrates at direct). Template saturation rare (2 of 400 cells at >0.8 cosine). Ghost-LLM inflated rate FLAT (6.1% → 5.3%). Top-20 ghost-entry list: Visa new-grad × 4 with LLM/fine-tuning asks, PNNL / DataVisor / PayPal AI roles mislabeled entry by YOE sparsity. Pattern-validation pipeline shipped validated_mgmt_patterns.json with 7 patterns at ≥0.92 precision (firm rebuilt from 0.54 → 1.00 by dropping document-noun "requirements"; hedging 0.92; scope_kitchen_sink 0.96; all 3 V1-rebuilt re-validated at 0.96-1.00). T23. Employer-requirement / worker-usage divergence Agent M ✅ ⚠️ T23.md Employer ai_strict 1.03% (2024) → 10.61% (2026) = +9.6pp / 10.3×. ai_any 5.07% → 29.36%. Worker-side already at 63-90% (SO 63→84, SO-daily 50.6, Anthropic 75 task-cov, DORA 90). Divergence direction ROBUST across 50/65/75/85 bands — 2026 gap: +39 to +74 pp under ai_strict. GitHub Copilot at 0.10% in postings vs ~33% regular-use industry benchmark — the MOST-adopted AI tool is the LEAST-formalized requirement. RAG at 5.2% (58×) is the leading tool-specific rise. Direction is consistent across cohorts, aggregator subsets, and seniority cells. Paper-lead candidate figure produced. V2 ⚠️: same pattern-label mismatch as T16 — report says ai_strict_v1_rebuilt but measured under top-level pattern. Under V1-rebuilt the ratio is 18.6× (0.75%→13.93%) rather than 10.3×; direction and divergence claim robust. T28. Domain-stratified scope changes by archetype Agent O ✅ T28.md Scope inflation is 60-85% WITHIN-DOMAIN. Aggregate breadth_resid Δ +2.07: within +1.27 (61%), between +0.56, interaction +0.24. Same within-dominant pattern for tech_count, scope_density, credential_stack, ai_binary. J3 entry-share rise Δ +5.07 pp is within-domain +6.84, between-domain −0.02 (domain recomposition does NOT explain the rise). AI/ML archetype grew 5.4× (601→3,262); tech stack shifted to LLM/agent (AI-agent 1.5%→33.8%, LLM 23%→52%, RAG 2.3%→26%); 50% new-vs-returning employer mix. Senior>junior breadth-gap widened in 9 of 13 domains; narrow in ai_ml (+0.49 vs +1.0 elsewhere). Arshkon-only magnitude J3 +1.17 / S4 −1.71 pp — within-dominant decomposition robust across pooled / arshkon / non-agg T30 panels. T29. LLM-authored description detection Agent O ✅ T29.md Hypothesis REJECTED as dominant mediator. Sig-vocab density rose (75% of 2026 postings contain ≥1 LLM tell vs 58% 2024, Cohen d=0.22, SNR=4.8). Wave 2 content effects PERSIST on low-sig-vocab bottom quartile: AI-binary Δ +0.115 vs +0.131 (88%), tech_count +1.99 vs +2.06 (97%), credential J3 +16.6pp vs +17.0pp (97%), scope_density +0.123 vs +0.096 (128%). Length growth is HALF-LLM-mediated (+583 vs +1,130 chars, 52%) — partial mechanism for boilerplate. T13 requirements-share decline near-disappears on low-LLM (−0.025 → −0.002) — adds authorship-sensitivity layer on top of V1 classifier-sensitivity (demote T13 H3). Methods caveat: sentence-length / em-dash / bullet features are format-confounded or within-2024-noise-dominated; only signature vocab density passes calibration in mixed-format longitudinal corpora.

docs.title: Wave 3.5 — Induced Hypothesis Tests
docs.text: Task Hypothesis Owner Status Report Key finding / note T31. Same-co × same-title drift H_M Agent Q ✅ ⚠️ T31.md Pair-level AI Δ +13.4 pp EXCEEDS T16 co-level +7.7-8.3 pp — within-firm AI rewriting is same-title, not title-recomposition. Pair-level breadth_resid Δ SHRINKS 90%+ vs T16 co-level (pair +0.08 pooled vs co-level +0.40 on 18 cos) — scope inflation is cross-title within-co, NOT within-title. V1-mgmt rises +12-16 pp at pair level (corroborates T21). Wells Fargo / Microsoft / Capital One dominate top-20 AI-drift exemplars (Wells Fargo lead SWE +52.9 pp, Microsoft SDE-II +40 pp at n=35). n=23 arshkon_min3, n=33 pooled_min5 at n≥3 threshold. Aggregator-exclusion TIGHTENS AI signal (+16.5 pp without agg). YOE at pair level drops ~0.3-1.0 yr — entry-ification visible at pair level. V2 ⚠️: exact n=23 pair count and +13.4 pp magnitude do not reproduce from documented methodology — V2 replication gets n=37 at +9.98 pp (relaxed) or n=12 at +13.3 pp (with strict arshkon-source-only 2024 filter). Direction (pair > company-level) robust; cite +10-13 pp range rather than single point. T32. Cross-occupation employer/worker AI divergence H_A Agent Q ✅ T32.md Direction UNIVERSAL 16/16 subgroups in BOTH 2024 AND 2026 under any-AI benchmark. Spearman(worker-mid, employer-2026)=+0.92 — employers rank occupations identically to workers; gap is in LEVEL (compressed 10-30%), not rank. SWE-concentrated magnitude: SWE-adjacent non-thin gaps 35-58 pp, control 15-50 pp. ml_engineer adjacent 63.1% 2026 ai_strict is ONLY subgroup where employer approaches worker. Nurse ai_strict=0.00% on 6,801 postings. Accountant worker 50% vs employer 0.69% = 72× gap. Control engineers 0.1-0.5% despite workers at 15-30%. 18 external benchmarks curated from SO/NBER/Anthropic/Thomson Reuters/CFA/ISC2. Paper-lead chart candidate. T33. Requirement-section change as hiring-bar signal H_B Agent R ✅ T33.md H_B REJECTED. Two-classifier comparison: T13 vs simple-regex give OPPOSITE signs on the period effect (T13 adjusted −0.019; simple +0.030; both HC3 p<1e-13). Within-2024 SNR for T13 req_share = 0.97 (at noise) — extends Gate 2's J3-specific classifier sensitivity flag to the AGGREGATE share shift. Hiring-bar proxy correlations within 2026 scraped uniformly |ρ|≤0.28, several sign-flipping across classifiers. Within-company Δ(req_share) × Δ(J3 share) ≈ 0 under both classifiers (n=356 overlap panel). Narrative 50 largest-contraction postings: 56% tech-req migration to responsibilities, 30% culture/benefits expansion, 0% explicit requirement-loosening language. Δ(req_chars) × Δ(desc_length) at r≈+0.35 supports "narrative expansion dominating" over "hiring bar lowering." T34. Emergent senior-role archetype profiling H_C Agent R ✅ T34.md H_C supported for both candidate clusters. Cluster 0 = Senior Applied-AI / LLM Engineer — genuine emergent role: 15.6× growth (144→2,251), 94% 2026, titles 28% AI Engineer, T09 cross-tab models/systems/llm 6.75× over-rep + systems/agent/workflows 5.65× over-rep; distinguishing bigrams claude code (+5.52), rag pipelines (+5.04), github copilot claude (+5.02), langchain llamaindex (+4.86), augmented generation rag (+4.72); median YOE 6.0 (vs 5.0 cluster 1); Software Development 45% + Financial Services 17%; HHI 39 across 1,163 firms; concentrates in T16 ai_forward_scope_inflator cluster at 30%. Cluster 1 = Senior Data-Platform / DevOps Engineer (less sharp — bundles DE + DevOps/SRE + AI-lab data-contract work): 2.6× growth, +11.4 pp share, orch 2.27/1K, T09 pipelines/sql/etl 2.62× + kubernetes/terraform/cicd 2.11×; concentrates in T16 traditional_hold clusters. Both clusters <3% robust to aggregator-exclusion. AI-oriented roles ask +1 YOE more than traditional senior — supports sharpened-seniority finding. T35. Technology ecosystem crystallization H_K Agent S ✅ T35.md Modularity +0.029 (0.632→0.662), n_comm 9→10, Louvain SD<0.002 across 10 seeds. LLM/AI cluster already existed 2024 (n=16 pooled); 2026 added mcp+gemini+chroma+claude_api (4 new arrivals) and SPLIT OFF an AI-dev-tools sub-cluster (copilot+cursor+chatgpt+claude_tool+codex; ⅘ members new). Observability merged into devops. Data-eng fragmented (Snowflake/dbt/Airflow vs PHP/Kafka/MongoDB). Domain-concentrated: LLM-vendor 9× in ai_ml, data-eng 3.3× in data_eng, ai_dev_tools FLAT across domains. Δ robust to aggregator-excluded (+0.022) and cap-20 (+0.029). T36. Legacy-stack substitution map H_L Agent S ✅ ⚠️ T36.md Scope-limited (6 of 11 widened disappearing titles match under default filter). Substitutions mid-cosine [0.30, 0.59]; all 6 are "engineer/developer" absorbing architect/specialist titles. Architect→engineer drops 2-4 YOE (title-inflation reversal, 0/6 upward). Content drift: SSIS/Unix-scripts/Drupal/PHP/Scala leave → Postgres/pgvector/CI-CD/microservices/Terraform/ArgoCD arrive. AI-vocab Δ is SMALL (source 0.00 → neighbor mean 3.6%, below 14.4% market rate) — "legacy→AI-enabled roles" NOT supported; substitution is legacy-stack-to-modern-stack only. T37. Sampling-frame returning-companies sensitivity H_H Agent T ✅ T37.md 13 of 15 Gate-⅔ headlines robust (ratio ≥0.80) on 2,109-co returning cohort; 1 partially robust (J3 breadth_resid 0.70); 0 sampling-frame-driven. J3/S4 directions INTENSIFY on returning (+6.2 pp vs +5.1 full; −8.3 pp vs −7.6 full). Within-co AI-strict +7.91 pp on T37 matches T16 arshkon_min5 +8.34 / pooled_min5 +7.65 within 0.45 pp — Gate-3 consolidated range +7.7 to +8.3 pp. S4 within-co decline (−9.31 pp) EXCEEDS aggregate (−8.29 pp) — parallel "exit-driven senior decline" mirrors T16's "exit-driven junior rise". T38. Hiring-selectivity × scope correlation H_N Agent T ✅ T38.md H_N rejected. Pearson |r|<0.11 on all selectivity-hypothesis metrics (breadth, ai_strict, scope, mentor, yoe) on arshkon_min3 n=243 primary. Only significant cross-period r is desc_len_median r=+0.20 (p=0.0015) — POSITIVE direction, opposite of selectivity prediction; strengthens to r=+0.29 on non-aggregator+non-giant subset (n=144). Seniority-stratified ai_strict Spearman r=+0.30 on J3, +0.20 on S4 (both p<0.01) — volume-UP firms AI-rewrite MORE, again opposite of selectivity. Robust across panel, size class, archetype, and within-2024 baseline. Paper can claim content shifts are demand-side/technology-mediated independent of JOLTS-2026 hiring cycle.

docs.title: Gate 3 Verification
docs.text: Task Owner Status Report Key finding / note V2. Gate 3 adversarial verification Agent V2 ✅ V2_verification.md All 13 tested Wave 3 + Wave 3.5 headlines verified on direction; 10 of 13 within 5% on magnitude. Two flags: (1) T16/T23 pattern-label mismatch — report text says ai_strict_v1_rebuilt but code uses top-level ai_strict; numbers match top-level pattern (report-text correction needed, not content error); (2) T31 pair count (n=23 claimed) does not reproduce from documented methodology — V2 gets n=37 (direction +9.98 pp) or n=12 under strict filter (+13.3 pp); pair-level > company-level direction robust. T18 SWE DiD survives all alt control defs (+13.1 pp under V1-rebuilt). T34 Applied-AI cluster content coherence confirmed via 20-row title sample (70% explicit AI/ML). T37 says 13/15 robust, saved table shows 14/15. T32 universality exact replication from saved output.

docs.title: Wave 4 — Integration & Synthesis
docs.text: Task Owner Status Report Key finding / note T24. Hypothesis consolidation Agent N ✅ T24.md 8 Wave 3.5 hypotheses tested (5 rejected, 3 supported); 6 T24-planned deferred (H_D highest priority); 3 new post-3.5 specs (H_O digital-maturity × AI-rewriting, H_P financial-services-compliance, H_Q J3 hedging-near-AI). Top-5 analysis-phase tensions identified; method-suitability assessed per tier. T25. Interview elicitation artifacts Agent N ✅ exploration/artifacts/T25_interview/README.md 9 artifacts + README mapping to RQ4 interview questions. Paper-lead candidate figures (T32 cross-occupation, T15 sharpening, T20 AUC, T34 archetypes, T31 pair-drift) + real posting exemplars (Microsoft SE II, Wells Fargo, Applied-AI cluster 0, ghost-entry, hiring-bar-contraction). T26. Exploration synthesis (SYNTHESIS.md) Agent N ✅ SYNTHESIS.md Paper's analytical backbone: 6 Tier A lead findings + 8 Tier B supporting + 8 Tier C methods + 5 Tier D flagged. Recommended positioning hybrid empirical/methods with A1 (cross-occupation divergence) + A2 (within-firm rewriting) co-lead. Integrates Wave 3.5 findings as first-class claims; includes robustness appendix (T37 + T38 + T29 + V1/V2), hypothesis status table (29 rows), method recommendations, and paper-figures candidate list.
